meanings.usage: 指停止工作，常见于职业生涯结束
形容引起广泛关注或震惊的事物
make up one's mind 表示下定决心
最高级形式，表示时间上最近的
指从事新闻报道工作的人

examples.en: I can't make up my mind.
She has made up her mind to go abroad.
They're going to get married next week.
Here's the latest report about Karen Marsh.
examples.zh: 这是关于卡伦·马什的最新报道。

phrases.phrase: Let me introduce him to you

examples.en: Let me introduce him to you. His name is Carlos.
examples.zh: 让我把他介绍给你。他叫卡洛斯。

examples.en: Are you really going to retire? I may.
examples.zh: 你真的打算退休吗？也许吧。

phrases.usage: 不让我...（表示禁止或不允许）

examples.en: He won't let me make another film.
examples.zh: 他不会让我再拍电影了。

phrases.usage: 真是...新闻，不是吗？（反意疑问句）

examples.zh: 真是轰动的新闻，不是吗？

phrases.usage: 我必须...（表示将来必要性）

examples.en: I will have to ask my future husband.
examples.zh: 我必须问问我未来的丈夫。

grammar.title: 直接引语转为间接引语 (Direct to Indirect Speech)
grammar.definition: 将别人的话用引述的形式重新表达，通常在人称、时态、时间状语和地点状语上需要变化
grammar.structure: 主语 + said (that) + 从句 / 主语 + asked + if/whether + 从句
grammar.usage: 用于转述他人的话语，要注意时态的倒退规则：一般现在时→一般过去时，现在进行时→过去进行时等

examples.en: She said, 'I may retire.' → She said (that) she might retire.
examples.zh: 她说：'我也许会退休。' → 她说她也许会退休。

examples.en: She said, 'I can't make up my mind.' → She said she couldn't make up her mind.
examples.zh: 她说：'我无法做决定。' → 她说她无法做决定。

examples.en: She said, 'He won't let me make another film.' → She said her future husband would not let her make another film.
examples.zh: 她说：'他不会让我再拍电影了。' → 她说她未来的丈夫不会让她再拍电影了。

examples.en: 'We will get married next week.' → She told us they would get married next week.
examples.zh: '我们下周要结婚了。' → 她告诉我们他们下周要结婚了。

grammar.title: 情态动词 may 的用法
grammar.definition: may 表示可能性，意思是"也许；可能"，用于现在时或将来时
grammar.structure: 主语 + may + 动词原形
grammar.usage: 表示不确定的可能性，语气比 can 弱，比 might 正式

examples.en: Are you really going to retire? I may.
examples.zh: 你真的要退休吗？也许吧。

grammar.title: 反意疑问句 (Tag Question)
grammar.definition: 陈述句后接简短问句，用来确认信息或征求同意
grammar.structure: 肯定陈述句 + 否定问句？/ 否定陈述句 + 肯定问句？
grammar.usage: 用于加强语气或确认对方观点，回答时根据实际情况选择 yes 或 no

examples.zh: 真是轰动的新闻，不是吗？

examples.en: She is a teacher, isn't she?
You don't like it, do you?

sentencePatterns.pattern: Are you really going to + 动词原形?

original.en: Are you really going to retire?

imitations.en: Are you really going to move to London?
imitations.zh: 你真的打算搬到伦敦吗？

imitations.en: Are you really going to buy that house?
imitations.zh: 你真的要买那栋房子吗？

sentencePatterns.pattern: I may / I can't make up my mind.

original.en: I may. I can't make up my mind.
original.zh: 也许吧。我无法做出决定。

imitations.en: Are you coming to the party? I may. I can't make up my mind.
imitations.zh: 你来参加聚会吗？也许吧。我无法做决定。

imitations.en: Will you accept the job? I may. I can't make up my mind.
imitations.zh: 你会接受这份工作吗？也许吧。我无法做决定。

sentencePatterns.pattern: He/She won't let me + 动词原形

original.en: He won't let me make another film.
original.zh: 他不会让我再拍电影了。

imitations.en: My parents won't let me go out tonight.
imitations.zh: 我父母今晚不让我出去。

imitations.en: She won't let me borrow her car.

sentencePatterns.pattern: That's + 形容词 + news, isn't it?

original.zh: 真是轰动的新闻，不是吗？
